payload.description: Presentation to Community Board 8 Transportation Committee about Utica Avenue Select Bus Service planning, including 2014 bus priority and safety improvements and proposed SBS upgrade for B46 Limited.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: The Utica Avenue B46 corridor was identified as a potential candidate for Select Bus Service in the 2009 Bus Rapid Transit: Phase II Study. Second-busiest bus route in NYC.
Second-busiest bus route in NYC, carrying nearly 50,000 passengers per day. Crucial connection to 3 4 A C trains. Connection to 30 bus routes including B35, B6, B12.
B46 Limited service to be upgraded to B46 SBS.
B46 Local service. Travel times decreased 8% to 15% after bus lane installation.

tool_args.label: 2014 Bus Priority and Safety Improvements - Utica Avenue

payload.description: Offset and curbside bus lanes on 1.3 miles of Utica Av corridor from St. John's Pl to Church Av. Left-turn restrictions and bay additions. Parking changes near Eastern Parkway and along southern part of corridor.

Proposed upgrade of B46 Limited to B46 SBS. Building on existing Bus Priority and Safety Project. May include off-board fare collection, additional bus lanes, transit signal priority, service planning changes, and capital improvements at SBS stations.

payload.description: Left-turn restrictions to improve pedestrian safety and left-turn bay additions to maintain traffic flow
+19 parking spaces near Eastern Parkway (within 3 blocks), -22 parking spaces along southern part of corridor (about 1 per block), new loading zones to accommodate businesses
payload.location_text: Near Eastern Parkway and southern part of Utica Av corridor

payload.description: Off-board fare collection proposed for Utica Avenue SBS
Additional bus lanes proposed for Utica Avenue SBS
Transit signal priority proposed for Utica Avenue SBS
Capital improvements at SBS stations including real-time passenger information, bus bulbs (sidewalk extensions at bus stops), new shelters, plantings, benches
Presentation to Community Board 8 Transportation Committee about Utica Avenue Select Bus Service

Step 1: Data collection and analysis. Monitor and evaluate 2014 project (bus speeds, vehicle travel time). Analyze full B46 corridor (traffic counts, parking survey, safety data, transit operations, ridership/transfer data, sources of delay, Local and Limited service patterns).

Step 2: Conceptual design. Identify feasible street design changes south of Church Avenue and north of St. John's Place. Develop conceptual service plans for B46 SBS and B46 Local. Solicit input from community members.

Step 3: Develop corridor plan. Develop detailed street design, prepare traffic analysis, identify SBS station locations, develop capital designs for station improvements, refine details with CAC, community boards, and general public.
Step 4: Final design plan. Finalize corridor design, develop implementation plan, potential 2015 SBS service launch, prepare construction phasing plan for capital elements.
CAC Meeting #2
Public Meeting #2
